title: Ladanella segetum
intro: Ladanella segetum, also known as daphne or spurge laurel, is a perennial, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its glossy leaves and clusters of small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to treat conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ladanella segetum include daphnetin, a lignan with reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Ladanella segetum is commonly prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
